place: East Rochester village, New York, New York

facts.text: East Rochester earned the nickname "Flour City" for its historic flour mills and "Flower City" for its thriving floriculture.
Frederick Douglass launched the abolitionist newspaper The North Star in Rochester in 1847, making the city a hub for civil rights.
German immigrants founded Bausch & Lomb in 1861, and George Eastman started Eastman Kodak in 1892, turning Rochester into a photography capital.
Xerox began as the Haloid Company in Rochester in 1906, later becoming a global leader in copying technology.
The former High Falls industrial area was converted into a tourist entertainment district in 1992, revitalizing East Rochester's waterfront.
